_comment: Per-chapter Scripture Gate. To pass a chapter the pilgrim must answer its key verse correctly; a wrong choice lets them choose again. answer = index of the correct option (spread across 0-3; ScriptureGate.gd also reshuffles display order each time). Each chapter's options are scene-specific with no cross-chapter reuse. Bilingual (zh primary, en in parentheses).

city_of_destruction.prompt: 传道者催你逃离将倾之城。挪亚因着什么预备了方舟,使全家得救?
city_of_destruction.prompt_en: Evangelist urges you to flee the doomed city. By what did Noah build the ark and save his household?
options: 因为城墙够厚 (Because the city walls were thick)
因为邻舍都没走 (Because no neighbor was leaving either)
因着信 (By faith)
因为生意还兴旺 (Because business was still booming)
city_of_destruction.correct: 「挪亚因着信……预备了一只方舟,使他全家得救。」(来 11:7)
city_of_destruction.wrong: 城墙、邻舍、生意都留不住将倾的城——救他全家的,是信。

wilderness_road.prompt: 旧城的声音仍在耳边。你当怎样奔那摆在前头的路程?
wilderness_road.prompt_en: The old city's voices still follow. How should you run the race set before you?
options: 存心忍耐,仰望耶稣 (With perseverance, eyes on Jesus)
边走边回望旧城 (Glancing back at the old city)
抄条近路绕过去 (Taking a shortcut around it)
等同伴齐了再起步 (Waiting until all companions gather)
wilderness_road.correct: 「存心忍耐,奔那摆在我们前头的路程,仰望为我们信心创始成终的耶稣。」(来 12:1-2)
wilderness_road.wrong: 回望、抄近路、空等都会停下你的脚——存心忍耐,定睛仰望祂。

slough_of_despond.prompt: 你陷在绝望的泥沼里。谁把你从祸坑、淤泥中拉上来,使你脚步稳当?
slough_of_despond.prompt_en: You sink in the mire of despair. Who lifts you from the miry pit and sets your feet firm?
options: 越挣扎越使劲 (By thrashing ever harder)
等淤泥自己变干 (By waiting for the mire to dry)
踩着易迁的肩 (By stepping on Pliable's shoulders)
耶和华 (The LORD)
slough_of_despond.correct: 「他从祸坑里、从淤泥中把我拉上来,使我的脚立在磐石上。」(诗 40:2)
slough_of_despond.wrong: 挣扎、空等、踩着别人都只会越陷越深——是耶和华把你拉上磐石。

wicket_gate.prompt: 窄门在前,指控如箭飞来。主应许:叩门的会怎样?
wicket_gate.prompt_en: The narrow gate is before you, accusations fly. What did the Lord promise the one who knocks?
options: 须先报上善行 (Must first recite his good deeds)
就给他开门 (It shall be opened)
要等守门人高兴 (Must wait for the keeper's good mood)
得另寻一道宽门 (Must look for a wider gate)
wicket_gate.correct: 「叩门,就给你们开门。因为凡叩门的,就给他开门。」(太 7:7-8)
wicket_gate.wrong: 不靠善行、不看脸色、也不必另寻宽门——只管叩,门就开。

cross_and_tomb.prompt: 在十字架前,你背上的重担滚落。这重担被谁担当了?
cross_and_tomb.prompt_en: At the cross your burden rolls away. Who bore that burden?
options: 走累了它自己松开 (It loosened as I tired on the road)
是空坟把它吸走 (The empty tomb drew it in)
主在木头上亲身担当 (Christ bore it on the tree)
是我哭够了才掉 (It fell once I had wept enough)
cross_and_tomb.correct: 「他被挂在木头上,亲身担当了我们的罪。」(彼前 2:24)
cross_and_tomb.wrong: 不是路途、空坟或你的眼泪卸下重担——是主在木头上亲身担当。

interpreter_house.prompt: 解经者用灯火照亮一间间屋子。什么是你脚前的灯、路上的光?
interpreter_house.prompt_en: The Interpreter lights each room. What is a lamp to your feet and a light to your path?
options: 神的话语 (Your word)
解经者手里的火把 (The Interpreter's own torch)
墙上那幅画 (The picture upon the wall)
我心里的预感 (A hunch within my heart)
interpreter_house.correct: 「你的话是我脚前的灯,是我路上的光。」(诗 119:105)
interpreter_house.wrong: 火把会灭、图画会留在屋里、预感会骗人——照亮路的是神的话。

hill_difficulty.prompt: 陡坡难行,力气将尽。等候耶和华的人会怎样?
hill_difficulty.prompt_en: The climb is steep and strength fails. What happens to those who wait on the LORD?
options: 一口气冲到山顶 (Sprint to the top in one breath)
坐着抱怨山太陡 (Sit and grumble the hill's too steep)
在凉亭里贪睡 (Doze on in the arbor)
如鹰展翅上腾,从新得力 (Renew strength, mount up like eagles)
hill_difficulty.correct: 「但那等候耶和华的必从新得力,他们必如鹰展翅上腾。」(赛 40:31)
hill_difficulty.wrong: 猛冲会力竭、抱怨与贪睡会误事——等候耶和华的,必从新得力。

palace_beautiful.prompt: 在华美宫你领受军装,前路有恶者埋伏。你当穿戴什么以站立得住?
palace_beautiful.prompt_en: At the Palace you receive armor. What must you put on to stand?
options: 美宫借给的华服 (The palace's borrowed finery)
神所赐的全副军装 (The whole armor of God)
姑娘们的几句祝福 (The damsels' parting blessings)
一把更长的剑 (A longer blade of my own)
palace_beautiful.correct: 「要穿戴神所赐的全副军装,就能抵挡魔鬼的诡计。」(弗 6:11)
palace_beautiful.wrong: 华服、好话、长剑都挡不住恶者的诡计——要穿戴神所赐的全副军装。

valley_humiliation.prompt: 亚玻伦以恐吓与控告压你。圣经说,务要怎样行,魔鬼就必离开?
valley_humiliation.prompt_en: Apollyon presses with threats and accusation. What does Scripture say to do, that the devil will flee?
options: 跟亚玻伦讨价还价 (Bargain terms with Apollyon)
假装没看见他 (Pretend not to see him)
顺服神,抵挡魔鬼 (Submit to God, resist the devil)
比他吼得更大声 (Try to out-roar him)
valley_humiliation.correct: 「故此,你们要顺服神。务要抵挡魔鬼,魔鬼就必离开你们逃跑了。」(雅 4:7)
valley_humiliation.wrong: 讲价、装看不见、硬比嗓门都没用——顺服神、抵挡他,他必逃跑。

valley_shadow_death.prompt: 你行过死荫的幽谷,黑暗与低语环绕。你为何可以不怕遭害?
valley_shadow_death.prompt_en: You walk the valley of the shadow of death. Why need you fear no evil?
options: 因为你与我同在 (For You are with me)
因为我闭眼快走 (Because I hurry with eyes shut)
因为低语吓不到我 (Because the whispers can't scare me)
因为天快亮了 (Because dawn is almost here)
valley_shadow_death.correct: 「我虽然行过死荫的幽谷,也不怕遭害,因为你与我同在。」(诗 23:4)
valley_shadow_death.wrong: 闭眼、逞强、盼天亮都壮不了胆——你不怕,是因祂与你同在。

vanity_fair.prompt: 集市以名利享乐相诱。圣经如何劝你待这世界与其上的情欲?
vanity_fair.prompt_en: The fair tempts with fame and pleasure. How does Scripture say to treat the world and its desires?
options: 货比三家再买 (Shop around for the best price first)
只看不买就好 (Just browse, never buy)
入乡随俗 (When in the fair, do as the fair does)
不要爱世界 (Do not love the world)
vanity_fair.correct: 「不要爱世界和世界上的事……这都是从世界来的。」(约一 2:15-16)
vanity_fair.wrong: 比价、空看、随俗都仍是被它牵着走——总归一句:不要爱世界。

doubting_castle.prompt: 巨人绝望把你囚在牢中。你手中『应许之钥』的力量从何而来?
doubting_castle.prompt_en: Giant Despair locks you in the cell. Why can the 'key of Promise' be trusted?
options: 指望巨人发善心 (Hoping the giant turns kind)
那应许我们的是信实的 (He who promised is faithful)
等牢墙年久自塌 (Waiting for old walls to crumble)
靠盼望力气比我大 (Because Hopeful is stronger than me)
doubting_castle.correct: 「也要坚守我们所承认的指望,不至摇动,因为那应许我们的是信实的。」(来 10:23)
doubting_castle.wrong: 巨人不会发善心、墙不会自塌、力气也不够——钥匙的能力在那信实的应许者。

delectable_mountains.prompt: 牧人们在喜山接待你,指给你看远方的圣城。谁是你的牧者?
delectable_mountains.prompt_en: The shepherds welcome you and show the distant City. Who is your shepherd?
options: 山上的好天气 (The fine mountain weather)
牧人手里的望远镜 (The shepherds' far-seeing glass)
耶和华是我的牧者 (The LORD, my shepherd)
我自己的脚力 (My own sturdy legs)
delectable_mountains.correct: 「耶和华是我的牧者,我必不至缺乏。」(诗 23:1)
delectable_mountains.wrong: 天气会变、望远镜是借来的、脚力会乏——牧养你的是耶和华自己。

enchanted_ground.prompt: 魔境的空气催人沉睡。圣经劝我们不要睡觉,却要怎样?
enchanted_ground.prompt_en: The Enchanted air lulls you to sleep. Scripture says: not to sleep, but to do what?
options: 要警醒谨守 (Watch and be sober)
坐下喝杯热茶 (Sit down for a warm cup)
数着脚步催眠自己 (Count steps until drowsy)
让盼望一人守夜 (Leave Hopeful to watch alone)
enchanted_ground.correct: 「所以我们不要睡觉,像别人一样,总要警醒谨守。」(帖前 5:6)
enchanted_ground.wrong: 歇脚、数步、把守望全推给同伴,都正中此地的圈套——总要警醒谨守。

river_of_death.prompt: 死河横在面前,水深可惧。你从水中经过时,凭谁的应许?
river_of_death.prompt_en: The river of death lies before you. Whose promise holds as you pass through the waters?
options: 指望河水自己退去 (Hoping the river recedes on its own)
等一座桥忽然出现 (Waiting for a bridge to appear)
比谁屏气更久 (Whoever holds his breath longest)
你从水中经过,我必与你同在 (I will be with you through the waters)
river_of_death.correct: 「你从水中经过,我必与你同在;你趟过江河,水必不漫过你。」(赛 43:2)
river_of_death.wrong: 水不会自退、桥不会自现、屏气也救不了你——凭据是祂与你同在的应许。

celestial_city.prompt: 天城的门为你而开。城里,神要为祂的子民成就什么?
celestial_city.prompt_en: The Celestial City opens to you. What will God do for His people there?
options: 按路程长短发奖牌 (Hand out medals by miles walked)
擦去一切眼泪,不再有死亡悲哀 (Wipe away every tear; no more death or sorrow)
给每人一座金库 (Give each a vault of gold)
让人睡到永远 (Let them sleep on forever)
celestial_city.correct: 「神要擦去他们一切的眼泪,不再有死亡,也不再有悲哀、哭号、疼痛。」(启 21:4)
celestial_city.wrong: 天城的福不是奖牌、金库或长眠——是神亲手擦去一切眼泪。
